description_long: This is a bulbous, earthenware vase with a narrow neck and a flared rim, featuring four rounded protrusions near the shoulder, each with a small hole at the center, possibly functioning as decorative handles or ornamental lugs. The surface is adorned with hand-painted motifs, including stylized fish in blue and yellow, crescent shapes, and simple floral or geometric patterns in dark blue and ochre. The body of the vase has a textured, matte finish with visible brushstrokes and a weathered, slightly uneven coloration, suggesting age or artisanal craftsmanship. The coloration is predominantly a warm, reddish-brown clay with accents of white, blue, and yellow. The interior appears hollow, consistent with traditional vases, and the base is rounded, designed to rest upright on flat surfaces. This object would be a striking decorative piece in an urban park, public plaza, or as part of a street art installation.
description: A hand-painted, bulbous earthenware vase with a narrow neck, four decorative lugs, and fish and floral motifs in blue, yellow, and ochre on a reddish-brown base. The surface is matte and textured, giving it an artisanal, weathered appearance.
description_view_0: Front view shows the vase's narrow neck, two prominent lugs, and a yellow fish motif with floral patterns on a reddish-brown background.
description_view_1: This angle highlights the back and side, revealing more of the blue fish motif and additional crescent and floral decorations, with all four lugs visible.
description_view_2: Side view displays a yellow fish motif and a clear view of the lugs, with the floral pattern encircling the shoulder of the vase.
description_view_3: Opposite side view features a blue fish motif and the same decorative lugs, with the floral and crescent patterns continuing around the body.
attribute_pattern: fish and floral motifs with crescent shapes
